idea.hook: Could an entire planet actually float?
facts: Saturn's average density is 0.687 grams per cubic centimeter (g/cm³).
Water's density is 1 gram per cubic centimeter (g/cm³).
Saturn is a gas giant with 95 times the mass of Earth and a diameter of over 120,000 km.
idea.payoff: If you could find a bathtub big enough, Saturn would majestically float on water!
idea.title: Saturn: The Floating Planet! 🛁🪐🤯

scenes.visual: Dark cosmic background with distant stars. A faint, stylized outline of Saturn. A large, glowing question mark appears in the center.
A detailed, slowly rotating Saturn against a deep space background. The density value appears with a subtle digital readout effect.

A stylized side-by-side comparison. Left: a clear beaker of blue water. Right: a translucent sphere showing wisps of gas, representing Saturn. Both are labeled with their respective densities, clearly showing Saturn's is lower.
Saturn, with its prominent rings, fills much of the screen. A tiny Earth is seen rapidly shrinking away in the foreground, emphasizing the immense scale difference.

scenes.text: Saturn would majestically float on water!
scenes.visual: A serene, vast, deep blue ocean surface under a starry night sky. A perfectly rendered Saturn gently bobs on the water, its rings extending elegantly above the surface.
